threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look stunned when the bus driver barely slows down for the stop. It’s like they think we’ll just teleport out.
Caught the 59 last night, and a guy tried to sell a half-eaten kebab. 'Gourmet leftovers!' he called it. Only in Brixton, I swear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old tram seat as a swing in the park—only in Neukölln do you find that mix of creativity and chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab place open at 3 AM blasting techno while serving the best late-night döner. It's a vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know when the burrito joint has run out of guac before even stepping inside. Tourists don’t check the specials and end up ordering the wrong thing.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo ride the 14 Mission during rush hour, completely unfazed by the chaos. Only in San Francisco...

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think they can just pop into a bar and find sangria, but most locals here prefer vermut on a Sunday afternoon. Totally different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle to order a simple 'café con leche' without an accent. Always asking for 'milk coffee' instead.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the 56 bus because you think it’ll take forever—locals know it’s the quickest way to get to the heart of Dalston.
Last night on the 47, a couple argued over the best kebab spot in the area. Turns out, the best ones are still tucked away behind the new flats.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I watched my neighbor argue passionately with the kebab vendor over the best spices. It felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to see a street musician serenading a crowd while a spontaneous dance-off breaks out. Pure Berlin chaos and creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their unflinching tolerance for the smell of burnt bread from Tartine—it's like perfume to them.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like watching a Muni bus halt for a street performer mid-song, only to see commuters nodding along like it’s part of the routine.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it’s all about the Sagrada Família, but the real charm is in those hidden plazas where locals sip coffee far from the tourist buzz.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they stare at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, despite the clear signs everywhere.
